macro_segments.title: Introduction to Daf 19 and Transition from Perek 1 to Perek 2: The Framework of Get Requirements

micro_segments.title: Overview of the masechet structure and the bifanai nichtav ubifanai nechtam parallels
Transition from bifanai nichtav to central aspects of ketivat haget
The previous mishna on nichtav hayom venechtam balaila and the question of dating requirements

macro_segments.title: Mishna: Physical Materials for Writing a Get - Permitted Inks and Surfaces

micro_segments.title: Bekhol kotvin - you can write with everything: bediyo, besam, besikarā, and all permanent things
Discussion of alternative girsa bedik khol davar shehu roshem and the meaning of permanence
Ein kotvin lo bemashkin velo bemei peirot - liquids and fruit juices cannot be used
Al hakol kotevin - you can write on everything: leaves, horns, and even on a slave's arm
Rabbi Yose HaGalili's dispute: don't write on living things or foods

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Analysis of Ink Types - Dyo, Sam, Sikra, Kumos, Kankantom and Their Permanence

micro_segments.title: Dyo as ink (dyuta) and sam as various plant-derived inks including arsenic and orpiment
Sikra as a reddish dye and kankantom as shoemaker's blacking, an iron compound
Kumos as gum arabic and the distinction between different permanent writing materials
Le'atuyei mai - what does bekhol davar shehu mitkayem include, like mei tarya and mei peirot

macro_segments.title: Gemara: The Case of Inking Over Previous Writing - Shabbat Comparison and Different Halakhic Areas

micro_segments.title: Hadyo al gabei sikra beshabbat - inking over red writing with black ink on Shabbat creates two transgressions
Kotev and mochek - is making black letters over red considered writing or erasing the red underneath
Sikra al gabei deyo - red on black, is it chayav for writing or exempt as mekalkel
Deyo al gabei deyo - fresh ink on old ink, no new writing if color is same

macro_segments.title: Gemara: The Case of Inking Over Previous Writing - Shabbat Comparison and Different Halakhic Areas (continued)

micro_segments.title: Introduction of the question about writing signatures on pre-inked names

macro_segments.title: Gemara: The Debate on Using Pre-Written Text for Witness Signatures - Analogy from Shabbat Limitations

micro_segments.title: Reish Lakish's question about edim she'ein yode'im lachtom writing over sikra and then signing
Rabbi Yochanan's response - ktav elyon ktav o eino ktav, is adding a layer new writing
The challenge from Shabbat law where ktav elyon ktav is considered chayav
Rabbi Yochanan's response: mipnei she'anu m'damim na'aseh ma'aseh - just because analogy doesn't mean rule
Discussion of why Shabbat and get signatures may have different criteria for ktiva

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Methods for Illiterate Witnesses - Stencils, Lead Writing, and Gallnut Ink

micro_segments.title: Rav's approach: mekra'im lahem niyar chalak - prepare a stencil for signatures
Shmuel's approach: ba'avar - pre-write with lead for them to write over
Challenge: if lead writing is valid ktav, how can they write over it
Resolution: distinction between actual lead (avar) and ink made from lead compound (mei maya d'avara)
Rav Abba's approach: bei mei milin - using gallnut juice solution that won't show
Ha de'afitz ha de'lo afitz - depends on whether klaf was worked with gallnut, affecting visibility

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Special Leniencies for Get Witnesses vs. Stringencies for Other Documents - The Agunah Exception

micro_segments.title: Rav Pappa's use of saliva (rok) for witness signatures and the limitation to gitten only
Vehanei milei be-gitten - these methods only allowed for gets, not other documents
Reasoning: agunah problem makes get necessary; danger of fraud for monetary documents
Story of Rav Kahana giving lashes to someone using stencils on non-get documents

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Literacy Requirements for Witnesses - Reading vs. Signing and the Problem of Abuse

micro_segments.title: Tanya kevateih de'Rav - Tanna Kamma allows both stenciled signatures and others reading the document
Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel's distinction: allows reading by others only by gitten, not other documents
Mai ta'ama - the reason is shelo yehu bnot yisrael agunot, emphasis on agunah problem
Halacha keRabban Shimon ben Gamliel - ruling follows this distinction
Rav Gamda's position allowing stencils by all documents and the rejection of this view

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Reading Requirements for Witnesses - Trust and Verification Methods for Non-Get Documents

micro_segments.title: Targema akria - clarification that by other documents we allow reading but not stenciled signatures
Stories of Rav Elazar and Rav Nachman having documents read to them before signing
Vedavka Rav Nachman vesafrey dayanim - only trusted readers like judges' scribes can be relied on
Concern about casual reading - potential for deception even with actual document present

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Foreign Language Documents and Verification Methods - Persian Get and Non-Jewish Courts

micro_segments.title: Rav Pappa and documents in Persian from non-Jewish courts - makrey lehu lishney kutim
Having two non-Jews translate separately to verify accuracy - bemasiach lefi tumo
Magbi bei mimeshabedi - trusting even liens on property based on translated foreign documents
Rav Ashi's report of Ameimar on Persian get written by Jews with Jewish witnesses
Concerns about forgeability - davar sheyachol lehizdayef - requiring ink that cannot be erased
Bi'defitzna - gallnut-treated parchment prevents erasure and allows Persian documents
Final distinction - kol lashon kasher, language does not matter if document is otherwise valid

topical_tags.term: ein kotvin lo bemashkin velo bemei peirot
